REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT AGNES HUEY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES ON PLAINTIFF RICCI CORNELLS THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT BY DEFENDANT AGNES M. HUEY, TRUSTEE OF THE SURVIVORS TRUST UNDER THE SUNG KWONG HUEY AND LAI YUNG HUEY REVOCABLE TRUST IS DENIED. AS TO THE FIRST ISSUE, THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHIFT ITS BURDEN AND SHOW THAT DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO A JUDGEMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW, DISPROVE AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OR SHOW THAT AN ELEMENT OF TRESPASS CANNOT BE ESTABLISHED. STONEGATE HOWMEONWERS. ASSN V. STABEN (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 740, 750. NONE OF THE THREE FACTS LISTED IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT ARE MATERIAL TO THE CHALLENGED ELEMENTS OF TRESPASS AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT, I.E. "UNAUTHORIZED ENTRY" AND/OR "CAUSATION" (NOTICE OF MOTION 2: 6-7). NO OTHER ELEMENTS DISCUSSED IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. AS TO THE SECOND ISSUE, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHOW THE ENTITLEMENT TO A JUDGMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW AS CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE AND NEGLIGENCE MAY BE ALLEGED IN THE ALTERNATIVE. SEE AUGUST 28, 2016 ORDER OVERRULING DEMURRER. NO FACTS LISTED IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT AS TO THIS ISSUE. AS TO THE THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH ISSUES, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE: MALICE, OPPRESSION OR FRAUD. SEE EX. C TO SELTZER DECLARATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues On Plaintiff Ricci Cornells Third Amended Complaint By Defendant Agnes M. Huey, Trustee Of The Survivors Trust Under The Sung Kwong Huey And Lai Yung Huey Revocable Trust
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Phillip Seltzer, 415-967-1970 for Plaintifff/Opposing party Ricca Cornell.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT AGNES HUEY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues On Plaintiff Ricci Cornells Third Amended Complaint By Defendant Agnes M. Huey, Trustee Of The Survivors Trust Under The Sung Kwong Huey And Lai Yung Huey Revocable Trust is DENIED. As to the first issue, the moving party failed to shift its burden and show that Defendant is entitled to a judgement as a matter of law, disprove an essential element or show that an element of trespass cannot be established. Stonegate Howmeonwers. Assn v. Staben (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 740, 750. None of the three facts listed in the separate statement are material to the challenged elements of trespass as alleged in the complaint, i.e. "unauthorized entry" and/or "causation" (Notice of Motion 2: 6-7). No other elements discussed in the Points and Authorities.
As to the second issue, Defendant failed to show the entitlement to a judgment as a matter of law as causes of action for nuisance and negligence may be alleged in the alternative. See August 28, 2016 Order Overruling Demurrer. No facts listed in the separate statement as to this issue. As to the third, fourth and fifth issues, there is a triable issue of fact re: malice, oppression or fraud. See Ex. C to Seltzer Declaration. Order signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
